summary: I reviewed the business requirements (`requirements.md`) for completeness and atomicity against the ask, the discovery notes, and the open-questions assumptions, and cross-checked the two prior critic verdicts. The requirements are well-structured — every menu item in the ask is mapped to a concrete "When X, Y" behavior and the human-vs-automatic-loop race is anticipated (R18–R21) — so I did not find blockers. But I emitted a `needs_work` verdict with five findings: three major gaps (no requirement that fresh menu-action outputs are surfaced to the person for review, the undefined re-pause behavior of the multi-step "Repeat from the Beginning" action, and the missing failure path for a menu-triggered re-run) and two minor ones (Done's validity before convergence given the existing gate precondition, and two non-atomic requirements R13/R23 that bundle independently testable behaviors). The verdict is written to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/requirements-completeness-critic/verdict.json`.
